description:
You are helping a small utility-script developer adjust their project configuration files.  
    In the project directory /home/user/project three files already exist:  

    1. /home/user/project/app.yaml  
       Current content:  
       ----------------  
       services:  
         database:  
           host: localhost  
           port: 5432  
       ----------------  

    2. /home/user/project/pyproject.toml  
       Current content:  
       ----------------  
       [tool.poetry]  
       name = "tiny-util"  
       version = "0.1.0"  
       description = "Small helper scripts"  

       [tool.poetry.dependencies]  
       python = "^3.9"  
       ----------------  

    3. /home/user/project/README.md (ignore for this task)  

    Goal  
    ----  
    a) Edit the YAML file so that a new top-level key logging is added **right after** the services key (the order matters).  
       The new logging section must look exactly like this (indentation: two spaces):  

       logging:  
         level: INFO  
         file:  /var/log/tiny-util.log  

       After your edit, the YAML file must therefore become:  

       services:  
         database:  
           host: localhost  
           port: 5432  
       logging:  
         level: INFO  
         file:  /var/log/tiny-util.log  

    b) Edit the TOML file so that the version field is bumped from "0.1.0" to "0.2.0".  
       No other lines may be altered or reordered.  
       The resulting TOML snippet must be:  

       [tool.poetry]  
       name = "tiny-util"  
       version = "0.2.0"  
       description = "Small helper scripts"  

    c) Create a plain-text report file named /home/user/project/changes_log.txt.  
       The report must contain exactly three lines in this order (no extra blank lines, trailing spaces, or BOM):  

       YAML updated: logging section inserted  
       TOML updated: version set to 0.2.0  
       Timestamp: <ISO-8601>  

       Replace <ISO-8601> with the current UTC timestamp in strict ISO-8601 format (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ).  

    Verification notes  
    ------------------  
    • The automated checker will compare the final contents of app.yaml, pyproject.toml, and changes_log.txt verbatim (byte-for-byte).  
    • The checker is whitespace-sensitive; be careful with indentation (two spaces) in the YAML and the exact placement of a single space after “file:” in the logging section.  
    • Only one terminal command is really needed, but you may use as many as you like.  
    • Do not remove or rename any existing file.  
    • Remember to work inside /home/user/project.
truth:
Initial filesystem before the task starts:
      Directory: /home/user/project
        app.yaml contents:
          services:
            database:
              host: localhost
              port: 5432

        pyproject.toml contents:
          [tool.poetry]
          name = "tiny-util"
          version = "0.1.0"
          description = "Small helper scripts"

          [tool.poetry.dependencies]
          python = "^3.9"

        README.md contents: "placeholder – irrelevant"

    Expected final state after correct task execution:
        /home/user/project/app.yaml EXACT bytes:
          services:\n  database:\n    host: localhost\n    port: 5432\nlogging:\n  level: INFO\n  file:  /var/log/tiny-util.log\n
          (i.e., UNIX newlines; two-space indents; a single space after "file:" before two spaces.)

        /home/user/project/pyproject.toml EXACT bytes:
          [tool.poetry]\nname = "tiny-util"\nversion = "0.2.0"\ndescription = "Small helper scripts"\n\n[tool.poetry.dependencies]\npython = "^3.9"\n

        /home/user/project/changes_log.txt:
          Line-1: "YAML updated: logging section inserted"
          Line-2: "TOML updated: version set to 0.2.0"
          Line-3: "Timestamp: 2024-06-15T14:30:00Z"   (the test harness injects a fixed timestamp; agent will supply any ISO-8601; harness will override for comparison)

    The automated test will:
        • Confirm app.yaml and pyproject.toml match the exact strings above.
        • Read changes_log.txt and verify the first two lines match exactly; the third line must start with "Timestamp: " followed by a valid ISO-8601 UTC timestamp.
